assets.alt: Flat head-on LinkedIn content operating board connecting sales questions to team expertise on a mint and soft green mesh background
assets.caption: LinkedIn content infrastructure turns field insight into public expertise sales can use.
assets.generation_method: built-in image_gen, saved post-local as PNG
assets.notes: Regenerated once to remove decorative connector-node motifs. Final uses a flat head-on three-panel operating board on a restrained mint and soft green mesh background.
assets.final_prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: wide blog hero for Sell In Public blog, final should work as a landscape PNG near 1897x829
Source context from article: The article explains how B2B sales teams should build LinkedIn content infrastructure as a weekly operating loop, not random posting. The system captures buyer questions from sales calls and customer conversations, matches those questions to credible internal experts, reviews posts for risk, publishes and comments on a steady rhythm, measures both platform signals and sales feedback, and repurposes the best answers into owned content and outbound assets. The central idea is sales-team expertise moving through a clean operating board into reusable public content.
Primary request: Create one simple visual metaphor inferred from the article source context: a flat operating board that connects captured buyer questions, team expertise, and reusable content. Use one relevant element only: one front-facing liquid-glass board divided into three simple panels. Do not mimic exact LinkedIn UI.
Style reference: Use the Sell In Public Flat Liquid Mesh Hero style: generated raster PNG, softly blended mesh gradient background using one main color plus at most one close complementary color, matte translucent liquid-glass shapes, simple head-on flat UI, consistent white outline weight, clean negative space, and subtle grain.
Composition: Wide landscape hero near 1897x829. Place one central board with quiet space around it. Keep the graphic readable as a single concept, not an infographic or screenshot. All glass/white graphics must be completely flat and viewed straight-on, like a 2D interface parallel to the image plane.
Scene/backdrop: Soft mesh gradient using only mint and soft green. No third color family. Blend colors smoothly without hard edges.
Subject: One simple front-facing flat liquid-glass board divided into three calm vertical panels. Left panel has three small abstract question cards made of non-readable white lines. Center panel has one simple outline person/profile mark with two short non-readable white lines. Right panel has two simple reusable content cards made of non-readable white lines. No top connector line, no circles, no node marks, no arrows, no network graphics, no random lines.
Detail density: Low. Simple, spacious, specific, editorial B2B.
Constraints: Generated raster PNG style only. No readable text, logos, watermarks, exact LinkedIn UI, fake metrics, numbers, charts, scattered nodes, node circles, connector lines, random lines, arrows, icon clouds, fake dashboards, overcomplicated UIs, generic LinkedIn-post-card default, glossy 3D objects, glow, bloom, flares, light trails, shiny/specular/reflection cues, bokeh/orbs, hard gradient edges, SVG/vector look, photoreal stock-photo look, more than two background colors, angled perspective, isometric view, tilted panels, 3D depth, shadows, or three-quarter UI view.
